title: lepidium eastwoodiae
intro: Lepidium eastwoodiae, also known as Eastwood's peppergrass, is a rare, perennial plant species in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is native to the Hawaiian Islands and has been used by local communities for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Lepidium eastwoodiae is believed to have health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and stems are used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas and infusions, which are said to aid in digestion and reduce fever.
